James' Morning Briefing

Monday, March 30, 2026

Seattle News

undefined° / undefined°
undefined
[object Object]

75,000-100,000 March in Seattle 'No Kings' Protest

An estimated 75,000 to 100,000 people marched through downtown Seattle on Saturday, March 28 as part of the nationwide 'No Kings' movement protesting the Trump administration. The protest started at Cal Anderson Park and marched to Seattle Center, tying up traffic on 4th and 5th Avenues until 4:30 p.m. Smaller rallies also took place in Ballard with approximately 500 people. Other Western Washington cities including Tacoma, Everett, Olympia, Bellevue, Bremerton, Federal Way, Issaquah, and Kirkland also held rallies.

undefined · Read more

Seattle Mariners Open 2026 Season

The Seattle Mariners opened their 2026 season on March 26 at T-Mobile Park against the Cleveland Guardians. The team entered Spring Training with one of their most complete rosters in recent memory. Notable absences from Opening Day include shortstop J.P. Crawford (right shoulder inflammation) and starting pitcher Bryce Miller (left oblique strain), both expected to return in April. Mariners games are now widely available across cable, satellite and streaming platforms for the 2026 season.

undefined · Read more

Seahawks News: Jaxon Smith-Njigba Signs Contract Extension

Seahawks wide receiver Jaxon Smith-Njigba visited the Virginia Mason Athletic Center on March 25, 2026 to sign a contract extension. The team is also preparing for the 2026 NFL Draft, holding the No. 32 pick. Mock drafts suggest the Seahawks may target cornerback or safety positions after losing Riq Woolen and Coby Bryant in free agency.

undefined · Read more

Orcas Spotted in Elliott Bay

Orcas were spotted in Elliott Bay off Duwamish Head on Sunday, March 29. According to reports, the whales appeared to be dining on prey in the area.

undefined · Read more

Seattle Symphony - Peanuts 75th Anniversary Symphonic Concert
Evening · Benaroya Hall ·
Join Charlie Brown, Snoopy, and the Peanuts gang for a one-of-a-kind symphonic experience celebrating the 75th anniversary of Peanuts. Features the legendary music of composer Vince Guaraldi with projected animations and live onstage actors.
